Title: Post-fix sanity: locked account remains locked after configurable max failed attempts
Preconditions: Account lockout threshold configured to 5; test user exists with PHI-safe credentials
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to login | 2. Submit wrong password 4 times | 3. Verify login still possible boundary not crossed | 4. Submit wrong password a 5th time | 5. Submit correct password
Expected_Result: After 4th failure account is not locked and accepts no further attempt only at 5th; at 5th failure account locks and correct password is rejected with generic lockout message; no PHI leaked

Title: Session timeout sanity after security patch redeploy
Preconditions: Session timeout configured to 30 minutes; user logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Log in | 2. Remain idle past configured timeout | 3. Attempt a privileged action (open patient chart)
Expected_Result: Session is invalidated, user redirected to login, no cached PHI rendered, re-auth required

Title: Sanity: duplicate-patient detection still fires after matching-rule change
Preconditions: Patient John Q Public exists; duplicate-detection enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Start new registration | 2. Enter matching demographics (same name+DOB+gender) | 3. Proceed to save
Expected_Result: Potential-duplicate warning is shown listing existing match; clinician must explicitly confirm before a second record is created

Title: Boundary: identifier check-digit validation at min and max identifier length
Preconditions: Identifier type with Luhn/mod-30 check-digit configured
Test_Steps: 1. Enter identifier one digit shorter than allowed | 2. Enter identifier at exact valid length with bad check digit | 3. Enter identifier at valid length with correct check digit
Expected_Result: Short identifier rejected; valid-length bad-check-digit rejected; valid identifier accepted and saved

Title: Negative sanity: search with SQL-meta characters returns no injection and no error
Preconditions: At least one patient indexed in search
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient search | 2. Enter a string containing SQL meta characters | 3. Submit
Expected_Result: Search returns zero or safe results, no server error, query is parameterized, audit log records the search without executing injection

Title: Sanity: phonetic/soundex match still returns expected patient after index rebuild
Preconditions: Patient surname Smith exists; search index rebuilt
Test_Steps: 1. Open search | 2. Enter a phonetic variant of the surname | 3. Submit
Expected_Result: Patient Smith appears in fuzzy results ranked appropriately

Title: Sanity: deceased patient banner and visit-start block persist after UI hotfix
Preconditions: Patient marked deceased
Test_Steps: 1. Open deceased patient dashboard | 2. Verify deceased banner | 3. Attempt to start a new visit
Expected_Result: Deceased banner displayed prominently; starting a new clinical visit is blocked or requires explicit override with reason captured in audit

Title: State transition sanity: active visit cannot be closed with unsigned orders
Preconditions: Active visit with one unsigned medication order
Test_Steps: 1. Open active visit | 2. Attempt to end/close the visit | 3. Observe validation
Expected_Result: Visit close is blocked or warns that unsigned orders exist; no order silently voided; state remains Active until resolved

Title: Boundary: backdated encounter datetime cannot precede patient birthdate
Preconditions: Patient DOB 1990-05-10; clinician with backdate privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Create encounter | 2. Set encounter datetime before DOB | 3. Set encounter datetime in the future | 4. Set valid datetime
Expected_Result: Pre-birth and future datetimes rejected with validation; valid datetime accepted

Title: Decision table sanity: pediatric vs adult BMI and abnormal-flag thresholds
Preconditions: Vitals form with age-aware reference ranges
Test_Steps: 1. Record height/weight for adult | 2. Record for pediatric patient | 3. Compare computed BMI and abnormal flags
Expected_Result: BMI computed correctly per formula; abnormal/high flags applied using age-appropriate percentiles for peds and absolute ranges for adult

Title: Boundary: physiologically impossible vital values rejected
Preconditions: Vitals form open on a patient
Test_Steps: 1. Enter pulse=0 | 2. Enter pulse=400 | 3. Enter temperature=20C | 4. Enter valid pulse=72
Expected_Result: Out-of-range physiologic values are blocked or require confirmation with a hard upper/lower bound; valid value saved

Title: Sanity: severe allergy drives interaction alert after drug-knowledge update
Preconditions: Patient has documented severe Penicillin allergy
Test_Steps: 1. Open ordering | 2. Order Amoxicillin | 3. Observe alert | 4. Order an unrelated drug
Expected_Result: Cross-reactivity allergy alert fires for Amoxicillin requiring override reason; no alert for unrelated drug; override captured in audit

Title: Sanity: coded diagnosis maps to correct ICD-10/SNOMED after concept-dictionary load
Preconditions: Concept dictionary loaded with mapped diagnoses
Test_Steps: 1. Add diagnosis by search | 2. Inspect saved coding | 3. Export via FHIR Condition
Expected_Result: Saved diagnosis carries correct SNOMED CT and ICD-10 mappings; FHIR Condition.code includes correct system URIs and codes

Title: State transition sanity: condition clinicalStatus active to resolved is auditable
Preconditions: Patient with an active condition
Test_Steps: 1. Open condition | 2. Change clinicalStatus to resolved with end date | 3. Verify history
Expected_Result: Status transitions to resolved, end date stored, prior active state retained in history, audit entry created with actor and timestamp

Title: Pairwise sanity: appointment booking across status, type and location combinations
Preconditions: Scheduling module configured with multiple types and locations
Test_Steps: 1. Book appointment varying type x location x status | 2. Save each | 3. Verify slot and provider
Expected_Result: Each combination books to correct slot/provider/location without double-booking; invalid combinations are prevented

Title: Negative sanity: double-booking same provider slot is prevented
Preconditions: Provider has one open slot at 10:00
Test_Steps: 1. Book patient A into 10:00 slot | 2. Attempt to book patient B into same provider 10:00 slot
Expected_Result: Second booking is rejected or routed to overbook flow with explicit confirmation; no silent collision

Title: State transition sanity: lab order lifecycle NEW to collected to resulted
Preconditions: Lab order placeable for patient with active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Place lab order (NEW) | 2. Mark specimen collected | 3. Enter result | 4. Verify each transition
Expected_Result: Order moves NEW->collected->resulted with timestamps; backward illegal transitions blocked; result links to original order

Title: Boundary: critical lab result triggers critical-value notification
Preconditions: Lab result entry with critical-range rules configured
Test_Steps: 1. Enter result just inside normal | 2. Enter result at critical-low boundary | 3. Enter result far above critical-high
Expected_Result: Normal value no alert; critical-boundary and beyond trigger critical-value notification and require acknowledgement; acknowledgement audited

Title: Sanity: radiology order accession number generated uniquely after sequence reset
Preconditions: Radiology ordering enabled; accession sequence configured
Test_Steps: 1. Place two radiology orders | 2. Inspect accession numbers | 3. Verify uniqueness and format
Expected_Result: Each order receives a unique, correctly formatted accession number; no collision after sequence reset

Title: Decision table sanity: dose range check by weight-based pediatric dosing
Preconditions: Weight-based dosing rules configured; pediatric patient with weight recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Order drug below min mg/kg | 2. Order at therapeutic mg/kg | 3. Order above max mg/kg
Expected_Result: Under-dose warns, therapeutic accepted, over-dose blocked or hard-stop with override+reason; calculations match mg/kg rule

Title: Negative sanity: dispense quantity cannot exceed ordered quantity
Preconditions: Active order for quantity 30 tablets
Test_Steps: 1. Open dispense | 2. Attempt to dispense 45 | 3. Dispense 30
Expected_Result: Over-dispense rejected with validation; valid dispense recorded and decrements remaining quantity

Title: Sanity: privilege revocation takes effect on next request after role edit
Preconditions: User has role granting Delete Patient privilege; admin available
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm user can access delete action | 2. Admin removes privilege from role | 3. User retries action in a fresh request
Expected_Result: After revocation the user is denied (403/no menu) on next request; no stale-cache bypass; denial audited

Title: Negative sanity: horizontal access to another patient via direct ID is blocked
Preconditions: Two patients; user without all-patient access scope
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as scoped user | 2. Open own-scope patient | 3. Manipulate URL/ID to another out-of-scope patient
Expected_Result: Out-of-scope access denied; no PHI rendered; attempt logged to audit with user and target

Title: Sanity: audit log captures view of restricted PHI record
Preconditions: Audit/logging enabled; restricted patient record exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the restricted patient chart | 2. Query audit log for the access event
Expected_Result: Audit entry records actor, patient, action=view, timestamp, source IP; entry is immutable/append-only

Title: Boundary: report date-range at inclusive start/end boundaries
Preconditions: Encounters exist exactly at range start and end dates
Test_Steps: 1. Run report with range covering boundary dates | 2. Verify boundary records included | 3. Shift range by one day and re-verify
Expected_Result: Records on inclusive start and end dates appear; records outside range excluded; counts match expected

Title: API sanity: Patient read returns correct resource after server patch
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; known patient UUID
Test_Steps: 1. GET /fhir/R4/Patient/{id} | 2. Validate status and structure | 3. Validate identifiers and name
Expected_Result: HTTP 200; valid FHIR Patient JSON; identifiers, name, gender, birthDate correct; meta.versionId present

Title: Negative API sanity: unauthorized FHIR request is rejected
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint requires auth
Test_Steps: 1. GET /fhir/R4/Patient/{id} with no/invalid token | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: HTTP 401/403; no PHI in body; OperationOutcome returned; attempt audited

Title: API sanity: Observation search by patient and code returns scoped bundle
Preconditions: Patient has vitals Observations with LOINC codes
Test_Steps: 1. GET /fhir/R4/Observation?patient={id}&code={loinc} | 2. Validate bundle
Expected_Result: HTTP 200 searchset Bundle; only matching patient+code Observations returned; total accurate; LOINC system URI correct

Title: Boundary API sanity: _count pagination at limit returns next link
Preconditions: More resources than page size exist
Test_Steps: 1. GET search with _count=1 | 2. Follow next link | 3. Verify no duplicate/overlap
Expected_Result: Each page returns at most _count entries; next link present until exhausted; no duplicated resources across pages

Title: HL7 sanity: ADT^A01 admit creates/updates patient via interface
Preconditions: HL7 v2 inbound interface configured
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A01 with PID and PV1 | 2. Verify ACK | 3. Verify patient/visit state
Expected_Result: Application ACK (AA) returned; patient created/matched; admission visit recorded with correct location/class

Title: Negative HL7 sanity: malformed message yields NACK not silent drop
Preconditions: HL7 inbound interface active
Test_Steps: 1. Send message with missing required MSH field | 2. Inspect ACK
Expected_Result: Application Error ACK (AE/AR) returned with error segment; message not processed; failure logged for reprocessing

Title: HL7 sanity: ORU^R01 result maps to correct observation and units
Preconditions: Lab interface configured with OBX mapping
Test_Steps: 1. Send ORU^R01 with OBX numeric result | 2. Verify mapped observation
Expected_Result: Result stored against correct patient/order; value, units (g/dL), and LOINC code mapped correctly; abnormal flag honored

Title: Security sanity: XSS payload in note field is neutralized on render
Preconditions: User can create a clinical note
Test_Steps: 1. Enter script payload into a note | 2. Save | 3. Reopen note in viewer
Expected_Result: Stored payload is escaped/sanitized; no script executes on render; raw value safely displayed; CSP enforced

Title: Security sanity: CSRF token required for state-changing POST
Preconditions: Authenticated session; state-changing endpoint
Test_Steps: 1. Capture a valid POST | 2. Replay without/with altered CSRF token
Expected_Result: Request without valid CSRF token is rejected (403); valid token accepted; rejection logged

Title: Accessibility sanity: registration form fully keyboard-navigable with visible focus
Preconditions: Registration form loaded
Test_Steps: 1. Tab through all fields | 2. Verify focus order and visible focus ring | 3. Submit using keyboard only
Expected_Result: All controls reachable in logical order, visible focus indicator present, form submittable without mouse, labels announced by screen reader

Title: Accessibility sanity: critical alert color contrast and ARIA role
Preconditions: An allergy/critical alert can be triggered
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger critical alert | 2. Inspect contrast ratio | 3. Inspect ARIA role/live region
Expected_Result: Alert text meets WCAG 2.1 AA contrast (>=4.5:1); alert uses role=alert/aria-live so it is announced; not color-only

Title: Performance sanity: patient chart loads within SLA after caching change
Preconditions: Patient with substantial history; performance SLA defined
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient chart cold | 2. Measure load time | 3. Reload warm
Expected_Result: Cold and warm load times meet SLA; no functional regression in displayed data; no N+1 spike in logs

Title: State transition sanity: critical alert acknowledgement clears active queue
Preconditions: An unacknowledged critical notification exists
Test_Steps: 1. View active alert queue | 2. Acknowledge the alert | 3. Re-check queue and history
Expected_Result: Acknowledged alert leaves active queue, moves to history with actor+timestamp; re-firing only on new trigger

Title: Decision table sanity: charge capture by visit type and payer combinations
Preconditions: Billing module configured with payers and charge rules
Test_Steps: 1. Close visit varying type x payer | 2. Inspect generated charges
Expected_Result: Correct charge codes and amounts generated per type+payer rule; self-pay vs insured adjustments applied; no duplicate charges

Title: Negative sanity: charge cannot post against a voided encounter
Preconditions: An encounter exists then is voided
Test_Steps: 1. Void an encounter with a pending charge | 2. Attempt to post the charge
Expected_Result: Charge posting is blocked for voided encounter; existing pending charge is reversed/voided; action audited

Title: Sanity: telemedicine visit captures consent before session start
Preconditions: Telehealth visit type enabled; consent workflow configured
Test_Steps: 1. Start telemedicine visit | 2. Attempt to join before consent | 3. Capture consent | 4. Join
Expected_Result: Session join blocked until consent recorded; consent stored with timestamp and method; then session proceeds

Title: Database sanity: patient merge preserves encounters and audit trail
Preconditions: Two duplicate patient records each with encounters; merge privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Initiate merge of duplicate B into A | 2. Confirm | 3. Verify encounters, identifiers, and audit
Expected_Result: All non-preferred encounters/observations re-pointed to preferred; identifiers retained; non-preferred voided not deleted; merge audited and reversible per policy

Title: Boundary sanity: bulk import rejects row exceeding field max length
Preconditions: Bulk import tool available; field max lengths defined
Test_Steps: 1. Prepare import with one over-length field | 2. Run import | 3. Inspect error report
Expected_Result: Over-length row rejected with row-level error; valid rows imported; partial-success report generated; no truncation silently applied

Title: Exploratory sanity: concurrent edit of same encounter by two clinicians
Preconditions: Two clinician sessions open the same encounter
Test_Steps: 1. Both open same encounter | 2. Both edit an obs | 3. Both save sequentially
Expected_Result: Optimistic-lock/conflict handling prevents silent overwrite; second saver is warned of stale version; no data loss; resolution path clear

Title: FHIR sanity: AllergyIntolerance criticality maps correctly cross-platform
Preconditions: Patient with high-criticality allergy; adapter layer active
Test_Steps: 1. Record severe allergy | 2. GET /fhir/R4/AllergyIntolerance?patient={id} | 3. Validate criticality and code
Expected_Result: FHIR AllergyIntolerance returns criticality=high, correct code system (RxNorm/SNOMED), clinicalStatus active; portable across adapters
